name: Security Audit
description: Security-focused development rules covering OWASP Top 10, input validation, authentication, secrets management, and dependency auditing
author: StartUpp AI IDE
rules: Validate and sanitize ALL user input on the server side, even if validated on the client -- never trust client data
Use parameterized queries or ORM methods for all database operations; never concatenate user input into SQL strings
Store secrets (API keys, database credentials, tokens) in environment variables or a secrets manager, never in source code or config files committed to git
Implement rate limiting on authentication endpoints and sensitive API routes to prevent brute-force attacks
Use bcrypt or argon2 for password hashing with a work factor of at least 10; never use MD5, SHA1, or plain SHA256 for passwords
Set HTTP security headers: Content-Security-Policy, X-Content-Type-Options, Strict-Transport-Security, X-Frame-Options
Validate JWT tokens on every request: check expiration, issuer, audience, and signature algorithm; reject 'none' algorithm
Apply the principle of least privilege: database users, API keys, and service accounts should have minimal required permissions
Run npm audit or equivalent dependency scan in CI/CD pipelines and block merges with critical vulnerabilities
Log security-relevant events (login attempts, permission changes, data access) with sufficient context for forensic analysis, but never log sensitive data like passwords or tokens

promptTemplates.name: Security Review
promptTemplates.description: Perform a security review of code

promptTemplates.name: Auth Implementation
promptTemplates.template: Implement secure {{authType}} authentication for a {{framework}} application. Include: password hashing with bcrypt, JWT token generation and validation, refresh token rotation, rate limiting on login, and proper error messages that do not leak user existence.

conventions:
Security conventions:
- All API routes require authentication unless explicitly marked as public
- Use .env for local secrets with .env.example committed as a template (no real values)
- Add a .gitignore entry for .env, *.pem, *.key, and credential files
- Rotate secrets on any suspected compromise, not just on schedule
- Document all public endpoints in the API specification
- Perform dependency audits weekly and before every production release
- Use HTTPS everywhere; redirect HTTP to HTTPS in production
